tests.name: test_final_state.py::test_deploy_succeeds
tests.trace:
@pytest.fixture(scope="module")
    def deployed_app():
        """Deploy the app, run it to populate Dict/Volume, then stop on teardown."""
        deploy_result = modal_cli("deploy", APP_FILE, timeout=300)
        assert deploy_result.returncode == 0, (
            f"modal deploy failed:\nstdout: {deploy_result.stdout}\nstderr: {deploy_result.stderr}"
        )
        run_result = modal_cli("run", APP_FILE, timeout=600)
>       assert run_result.returncode == 0, (
            f"modal run failed:\nstdout: {run_result.stdout}\nstderr: {run_result.stderr}"
        )
E       AssertionError: modal run failed:
E         stdout: 
E         stderr: ╭─ Error ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╮
E         │ Specify a Modal Function or local entrypoint to run. E.g.                    │
E         │ > modal run /home/user/hp_sweep/app.py::my_function [..args]                 │
E         │                                                                              │
E         │ '/home/user/hp_sweep/app.py' has the following functions and local           │
E         │ entrypoints:                                                                 │
E         │ evaluate_config / app.evaluate_config                                        │
E         │ run_sweep / app.run_sweep                                                    │
E         ╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
E         
E       assert 1 == 0
E        +  where 1 = CompletedProcess(args=['modal', 'run', '/home/user/hp_sweep/app.py'], returncode=1, stdout='', stderr="╭─ Error ──────...                                │\n╰──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯\n").returncode

tests/test_final_state.py:29: AssertionError
